hero.title: 6 Days in the Kingdom of Happiness.
hero.titleAccent: The Sweet Spot for First-Time Visitors to Bhutan.
hero.intro: The most popular Bhutan package from Malaysia — and for good reason. Six days gives you time to do everything properly: two nights in Thimphu with extended cultural exploration, the Wangditse nature trail through apple orchards and prayer-flag forests, a genuine farmhouse stay, and the legendary Tiger's Nest hike, without the rush that comes with five days.
hero.heroImageAlt: Bhutan Kingdom of Happiness Thimphu Valley Buddha Dordenma aerial view

includes: Meet & greet at Paro International Airport
Meals as per itinerary (B = Breakfast, D = Dinner)
All tours and sightseeing as per itinerary

overview.heading: 6 Days Bhutan Kingdom of Happiness: The Full Picture
body: Six days is the sweet spot for Bhutan. It gives you the time to move slowly, to sit with a monastery view and not be counting minutes, to linger over a meal with your farmhouse family rather than rushing to the next site. This is the most popular Bhutan package among Malaysian first-time visitors, and it earns that reputation.

The experience begins with a traditional khaddar greeting at Paro — a white silk scarf offered as a sign of welcome and respect. From there, two nights in Thimphu allow deep exploration of the capital: the 52-metre Buddha Dordenma, the monastic fortress of Tashichho Dzong, the National Memorial Chorten surrounded by morning prayers, and the optional Wangditse Nature Trail — a beautiful forest hike through apple orchards to a ridge monastery with panoramic valley views.

The middle days bring you to Paro for sacred heritage — Simtokha Dzong, Paro Rinpung Dzong, the Ta Dzong National Museum — and a full afternoon at a local farmhouse where you cook Ema Datshi and try archery. The final full day delivers the iconic Tiger's Nest Monastery hike, followed by a restorative traditional hot stone bath. Six days, no compromise, no rush.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers Choose the 6-Day Kingdom Experience

items.title: Wangditse Nature Trail — The Hidden Gem
items.body: Most Bhutan tours skip this. A two-hour forest hike from Thimphu through apple orchards, traditional farmhouses, and stands of blue pine wrapped in prayer flags, ending at Wangditse Monastery on a ridge above the valley. The views of Thimphu from the trail are extraordinary — the kind of views that make you stop walking and just stand there. The monastery at the top is atmospheric and quiet, far removed from the main tourist circuit.
items.imageAlt: Wangditse Nature Trail Thimphu Bhutan apple orchards monastery forest hike

items.title: Two Full Days in Thimphu — The Capital Without Traffic Lights
items.body: The 6-day package gives you a complete Thimphu experience that the 5-day simply cannot. Two nights and almost two full days to cover the Buddha Dordenma, Tashichho Dzong, National Memorial Chorten, Motithang Takin Preserve, the traditional textile weaving centres, and the Thimphu weekend market. You leave Thimphu having genuinely understood it, not just photographed it.
items.imageAlt: Thimphu capital Bhutan Buddha Dordenma Tashichho Dzong cultural tour

items.title: Farmhouse Stay — Cooking, Archery & Authentic Connection
items.body: The farmhouse experience in Paro is the moment most visitors describe when they talk about Bhutan months or years later. A local family hosts you for an afternoon and early evening — archery on traditional grounds, vegetable harvesting from the kitchen garden, cooking Ema Datshi (Bhutan's national dish of chilli and local cheese) from scratch. The family feeds you the dinner you just cooked. It is genuinely warm, not staged.
items.imageAlt: Bhutanese farmhouse experience archery cooking Ema Datshi Paro cultural immersion

items.title: Tiger's Nest Monastery — Unhurried on Day 5
items.body: Because the 6-day itinerary places the Tiger's Nest hike on Day 5, you arrive at it rested and unhurried. No cramming multiple sites into the same day. Just the forest trail, the cliff monastery hanging in the air 900 metres above the valley, and the silence of the meditation caves where Guru Rinpoche retreated in the 8th century. Followed by a traditional Menchu hot stone bath — one of the most restorative experiences Bhutan offers.
items.imageAlt: Tiger's Nest Taktsang Monastery Bhutan Paro Valley cliff face sacred site

itinerary.heading: Your 6-Day Bhutan Kingdom of Happiness Itinerary

days.title: Arrival Paro — Journey to Thimphu
days.description: Arrive at Paro International Airport and receive your traditional khaddar (silk greeting scarf) from your Dhesu guide. Drive to Thimphu via the scenic valley road, stopping at Tamchog Lhakhang — a 14th-century monastery beside the Paro River, connected to the road by one of Thangtong Gyalpo's famous iron chain bridges. Check in and evening at leisure in Thimphu.

days.title: Thimphu Valley — Heritage & Living Traditions
days.description: Full day in Thimphu. Begin at the Buddha Dordenma statue on Kuenselphodrang hill — 52 metres of gilded bronze, housing 125,000 smaller Buddha statues inside, with views over the entire Thimphu Valley. National Memorial Chorten for morning prayers and circumambulation. Motithang Takin Preserve to see Bhutan's unique national animal. Afternoon at Tashichho Dzong — the seat of government and one of the most beautiful examples of dzong architecture in the kingdom. Optional: Gagyel Lhundrup Weaving Centre for traditional textiles.

days.title: Thimphu — Wangditse Nature Trail & Cultural Exploration
days.description: Morning hike on the Wangditse Nature Trail — a two-hour forest walk through apple orchards, farmhouses, and prayer-flag draped pines to Wangditse Monastery on the Thimphu ridge. The trail is optional for those who prefer a gentle morning; the alternative is the Folk Heritage Museum or the Institute for Zorig Chusum (traditional arts school). Afternoon at the Thimphu weekend market or Tashichhoe Dzong interior, before dinner and evening in the capital.
suggestedActivities: Institute for Zorig Chusum (13 traditional arts)

days.title: Thimphu to Paro — Heritage & Farmhouse Experience
days.description: Morning visit to Simtokha Dzong — the oldest surviving dzong in Bhutan (1629), functioning as a monastic school. Drive to Paro through mountain valleys and rural farmland. Afternoon farmhouse cultural experience: archery with traditional recurve bows, vegetable harvesting, cooking Ema Datshi with the family. Evening: Paro Rinpung Dzong with views over the valley, and Ta Dzong National Museum.
suggestedActivities: Cook Ema Datshi with local family

days.title: Taktsang Monastery Hike — Tiger's Nest
days.description: The day Bhutan is famous for — and why the 6-day package puts it on Day 5 (rested, unhurried, fully immersed). An early morning hike through pine and cedar forest to Taktsang Monastery, clinging to a cliff face 900 metres above Paro Valley. Three hours up through improving views, a café at the halfway viewpoint, and the final dramatic section across a suspension bridge over a waterfall to reach the monastery complex. After descending, a traditional Menchu hot stone bath: river stones heated red-hot over an open fire, then dropped into a wooden tub with mountain herbs for a deeply restorative soak.
suggestedActivities: Tiger's Nest Monastery hike (3 hours up)

days.title: Farewell and Depart Paro
days.description: Final morning in Paro for last-minute shopping — thangka paintings, traditional handwoven textiles, yak wool products, incense, and Bhutanese stamps are popular purchases. Transfer to Paro International Airport accompanied by your guide.
suggestedActivities: Final photos at Paro town bridge

Six days at the right pace, with the right guide, in the right hotels — this is what 35 years of Bhutan experience produces.

sections.heading: 6 Day Bhutan Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 6-day Bhutan tour from Malaysia is the most popular duration for first-time visitors, and the right choice for anyone who wants to see everything without feeling rushed. This Kingdom of Happiness package covers Paro, Thimphu, Tiger's Nest Monastery, the Wangditse Nature Trail, a traditional farmhouse experience, and a hot stone bath — all at the pace Bhutan deserves.

The sixth day makes the difference. The 5-day package works, but you notice the rush on Days 3 and 4. With six days, every site gets the time it deserves, and you arrive at Tiger's Nest on Day 5 rested and ready — not exhausted from a packed schedule.

sections.heading: What Is the Bhutan Sustainable Development Fee?
sections.body: Bhutan charges all international visitors (except Indian, Bangladeshi and Maldivian nationals) a Sustainable Development Fee (SDF) of USD 100 per person per night. This fee funds free healthcare and education for all Bhutanese citizens, and supports the kingdom's conservation and environmental programmes. It is part of Bhutan's 'high value, low impact' tourism philosophy. The SDF is separate from the visa fee and tour package cost. Dhesu provides clear guidance on the current SDF rates and how they apply to your specific travel dates.

faq.heading: Questions About the 6-Day Kingdom of Happiness Package

items.question: Why is this the most popular Bhutan package?
items.answer: Six days is the right amount of time for Bhutan. It's enough to see all the iconic sites — Tiger's Nest, Thimphu's cultural highlights, Paro heritage, farmhouse experience — without rushing between each one. The extra day compared to the 5-day package is specifically used for the Wangditse Nature Trail and more relaxed Thimphu exploration. Most Malaysian visitors who have done both say the 6-day pacing is noticeably better.

items.question: What is the Wangditse Nature Trail?
items.answer: Wangditse is a 2-hour forest trail from Thimphu that most tour packages skip. It passes through apple orchards, traditional farmhouses adorned with prayer flags, and stands of blue pine before emerging at Wangditse Monastery on a ridge above the Thimphu Valley. The views from the trail and monastery are some of the best in the area. It's a gentle hike suitable for most fitness levels, and a highlight of the 6-day experience.

items.question: What is included in the traditional khaddar greeting?
items.answer: On arrival at Paro Airport, your Dhesu guide presents you with a khaddar — a white silk ceremonial scarf — as a gesture of welcome and respect. This is the traditional Bhutanese greeting for honoured guests. The khaddar is yours to keep as a memento of your first moments in Bhutan.

items.question: Can I do the Tiger's Nest hike if I am not very fit?
items.answer: Yes, with some preparation. The hike is approximately 10 km return with 900 metres of elevation gain. Most people of average fitness can complete it. Horse riding is available for the first 45 minutes of the ascent (up to the halfway café). Your guide will set a comfortable pace and there are rest points along the trail. The monastery is accessible to almost all visitors — it is the pace that determines difficulty, not the absolute challenge.
